observations.value: POST /session with any non-empty identifier and secret returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a 'Welcome' home page, regardless of credential validity. E.g., POST /session with identifier=wrong@example.com&secret=wrongpassword returns 200 and sets session=ok. Only completely empty credentials (identifier=&secret=) are rejected with 401. This means authentication accepts any non-empty credential pair as valid.

POST /order with a valid card number always returns HTTP 200 with 'Order #A1024 confirmed.' — the order confirmation number is always the same static value (#A1024) rather than a unique per-order identifier. Multiple successive POST /order requests all produce 'Order #A1024 confirmed.'

POST /order with a valid card number (e.g. card=4111111111111111) succeeds (HTTP 200 'Order confirmed') without requiring any session cookie or authentication. The /order endpoint does not enforce authentication.
GET /logout returns HTTP 404 (Not Found). The page rendered after a successful login at GET /session response shows a 'Sign out' link pointing to /logout, but that route does not exist.

GET /search?q= (empty query string) returns HTTP 200 with the blank search form rather than showing 'No results found.' — only GET /search?q=<non-empty> triggers the results page (e.g. /search?q=test returns 200 with 'No results found.'). An empty-string query silently shows the form instead of results.
